parameters.content: =Thanks for your interest — here's a quick-start guide.
=✅ Log form submissions with Google Drive and Discord 24/7 — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

### New Lead Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Company, Score). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Score the Lead

### Score the Lead

Custom JavaScript: Score each lead 0-100 from simple heuristics

Next: Assign Round-Robin Owner

### Assign Round-Robin Owner

Custom JavaScript: Round-robin owner assignment

Next: Route by Heat

### Route by Heat

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: hot, warm + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping · ②→ Warm → Drip via Discord · ③→ Cold → Park in Nurture List

### Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping

Upserts a row into 'Lead Tracker', matched on `leadId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Next: Notify Assigned Owner

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping

parameters.content:
### Notify Assigned Owner

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

Next: Combine Lead Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Warm → Drip via Discord

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

Next: Combine Lead Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Cold → Park in Nurture List

Appends a new row to 'Lead Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Next: Combine Lead Paths

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Cold → Park in Nurture List

parameters.content:
### Combine Lead Paths

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Send Summary to Discord

### Send Summary to Discord

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

# Log form submissions with Google Drive and Discord 24/7

**Pattern:** Form Intake · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Log form submissions with Google Drive and Discord 24/7. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual enrichment is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Lead Form Submission → Score the Lead → Assign Round-Robin Owner → Route by Heat → Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping → Notify Assigned Owner → Combine Lead Paths → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to form intake without manual effort
- Anyone automating enrichment in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Scales without extra headcount
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Round-robin owner assignment happens right after scoring, before routing — every lead gets an accountable owner regardless of tier, so even a 'cold' lead parked in the nurture list is someone's responsibility later, not an anonymous row in a spreadsheet.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Change the hot/warm threshold values and watch the tier distribution shift.
2. Add a 4th owner to the round-robin pool.
3. Add a follow-up wait+check to the warm-drip branch, similar to the drip-ladder spine.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your enrichment process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
